//: Editability-Policy PreToolUse guard — .claude/settings.json fragment.
WHAT IT DOES: before Claude Code runs a Write / Edit / NotebookEdit, this
hook resolves the target file to its editability level (via arch_gate.py)
and, if the file belongs to a protected component (conditional/never/frozen)
or contains a never/frozen arch:begin marker region, blocks the edit with a
prescriptive message (exit 2). It FAILS OPEN on any internal error, so a
broken hook can never stop you from editing. This is ergonomics only — the
CI gate (arch_gate.py on the PR) is the real, un-bypassable enforcement.
INSTALL:
1. Merge the "hooks" block below into your repo's .claude/settings.json
(create the file if absent). If you already have hooks.PreToolUse,
append this one matcher-object to that existing array.
2. Use an ABSOLUTE path to the hook script, or a "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"-
relative one as shown (Claude Code expands $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR to the
project root). The 'python3' launcher keeps it stdlib-only / no venv.
3. arch.policy.json is found by walking up from the edited file (or set
the ARCH_POLICY env var to an absolute path).
OPTIONS (env, all optional):
ARCH_GUARD_MODE=block  (default) exit 2 and block on a protected target.
ARCH_GUARD_MODE=warn   print the message but allow the edit (advisory).
ARCH_POLICY=/abs/arch.policy.json   pin the policy file explicitly.
ARCH_GUARD_DEBUG=1     extra diagnostics on stderr.
To also protect the hook itself from the agent (SPEC §7.3), mark this
script's path 'frozen' in a redline.meta.json — but remember a hook is
per-agent; the CI gate remains the guarantee.
